articles.title: GSAP v3.13: JavaScript Animation Set Free
articles.description: Last year the popular GSAP (a.k.a. GreenSock) animation library was acquired by Webflow and as of this new version the entire GSAP toolkit is freely available (including formerly paid addons like MorphSVG and SplitText) even for commercial use. If you're unfamiliar with GSAP and want to see some of what it can do, they have a showcase, lots of code demos, and amazing docs.
articles.author: Cassie Evans and Jack Doyle

articles.title: Converting Values to Strings in JavaScript
articles.description: When Dr. Axel says “converting values to strings in JavaScript is more complicated than it might seem”, I’m inclined to believe him. An interesting poke about into something seemingly simple you might not think about very much.
articles.author: Dr. Axel Rauschmayer

articles.title: 📉 Giving V8 a Heads-Up: Faster Startup with Explicit Compile Hints
articles.description: A look at a V8 optimization that lets you direct V8 to eagerly compile specific files as a way to speed up startup. It ships with Chrome 136 and the performance gains are more compelling than you might expect.
articles.author: Marja Hölttä

articles.title: Export Google Analytics Data to Google Sheets via Apps Script
articles.description: Google Apps Script is a JavaScript-based platform for dynamically automating tasks in all sorts of Google apps. Here’s how to use it to bring Google Analytics data into a Google Sheet.
articles.author: Kayce Basques

articles.title: Building an Offline-Friendly Image Upload System
articles.description: How to leverage PWA tech such as IndexedDB, service workers, and the Background Sync API to help improve the reliability of web apps for users, particularly those with unreliable internet connectivity.
articles.author: Amejimaobari Ollornwi

articles.title: What Does React's 'use client' Do?
articles.description: Dan Abramov takes a practical look at how to think about the use client and use server directives React Server Components introduced and how they let you elegantly structure a client/server app as “a single program spanning two environments” – a concept that Dan thinks could be more widely used, even outside of React.
articles.author: Dan Abramov

articles.title: PDFSlick 3.0: View and Interact with PDF Documents in JS Apps
articles.description: A full-featured PDF viewer for React, Solid, Svelte and vanilla JS apps. Built on top of PDF.js, it offers a wide array of features from simple PDF viewing to working with multiple and large documents with annotations. Demo. v3.0 bumps up to PDF.js v5 with ICC profile support, better JPEG 2000 support, and improved rendering of large pages.
articles.author: Vancho Stojkov

articles.title: Koa 3.0: The Expressive HTTP Middleware Framework
articles.description: Koa first appeared a decade ago as a ‘next-generation’ Web framework that shared some of the lineage of Express.js, but leaning on more modern JS features. While Express has been making a comeback, Koa remains a compelling alternative.
articles.author: Koa contributors

articles.title: Seyfert: A Framework for Building Discord Bots
articles.description: Create bots for the popular chat system starting from bots that respond to simple commands up to creating components and getting user input. Supports Deno, Bun, and Node.
articles.author: socram03

articles.title: Storybook 9 Beta
articles.description: A strong step forward for the UI ‘frontend workshop’ with several experimental features from Storybook 8 becoming stable. v9 has a strong focus on component testing, plus React Native support.
articles.author: Michael Shilman

articles.title: PGlite 0.3: Postgres but in WebAssembly
articles.description: A WebAssembly-based build of the Postgres SQL database meaning you can run it wherever WebAssembly can be run (such as in the browser as in this demo).
articles.author: ElectricSQL

articles.description: Node.js's releases are currently shifting around a little. v18 has just gone 'end of life', v23 has entered 'maintenance' mode and v24 is soon to land as the new 'current' release.
🤠 jQuery was first released in 2006 and to celebrate its 20th anniversary, there's a jQuery Reunion event in Texas on January 16-17, 2026.
The Svelte team has a look at what's new in Svelte lately, and the Astro team has a similar update for Astro too.
You can now install JSR packages with pnpm and Yarn.
Learn about JavaScript's numerous operators with a handy online reference.
Deno 2.3 – The alternative JavaScript runtime improves its single binary compilation feature to support FFI and Node native addons.
Prisma 6.7 – Popular ORM for Node.js and TypeScript.
Bun v1.2.11, Electron 36, pnpm 10.10

articles.title: 'Don't Make Google Sell Chrome'
articles.description: There have been rumblings about Google possibly having to sell off Chrome for a little while now — here, DHH (creator of Rails, Basecamp, etc) weighs in, noting that the web could be worse off if it comes to pass: “Google should not get away with rigging the online ad market, but forcing it to sell Chrome will do great damage to the web”.
articles.author: David Heinemeier Hansson

articles.title: Polishing Your Typography with Line Height Units
articles.description: Jen explains how the lh and rlh units give us a direct way “to tie any size in our layout to the vertical rhythm of the text” — be that margins, padding, gaps, width, height, etc.
articles.author: Jen Simmons (WebKit)

articles.title: Creating a 3D Split-Flap Display Effect
articles.description: A split-flap display is a electromechanical display commonly associated with live timetable displays and it makes for a neat effect on the Web too. Jhey breaks down how to replicate it or you can hit up this live demo.
articles.author: Jhey Tompkins

articles.title: Seeking an Answer: Why Can’t HTML Alone Do Includes?
articles.description: Plenty of solutions, but no straightforward, performant HTML one. This has been a tricky issue for decades now and gets worked around in numerous ways whether with JavaScript, a build step, IFRAMEs, or more. There is no answer here, but it's a good question.
articles.author: Chris Coyier

articles.title: How to Write Error Messages That Actually Help Users Rather Than Frustrate Them
articles.description: Solid, straightforward advice on creating error messages that help users recover quickly and continue their path through your products/services.
articles.author: Amy Hupe

articles.title: The <canvas> Element
articles.description: Highlights the accessibility pitfalls and performance trade-offs of using the <canvas> element, along with noting when it’s actually suitable to turn to it.
articles.author: Heydon Pickering

articles.title: ▶  🦉 What is the 'Owl Selector' (* + *)?
articles.description: A quick look at the so-called CSS ‘owl’ selector, including how it works, why you should use it, and the viable alternatives.
articles.author: Zoran Jambor

articles.title: Anchor Positioning Just Don't Care About Source Order
articles.description: Overlapping elements in CSS can often be tricky, but CSS anchor positioning should simplify things. Geoff shares how, noting that it adds another welcome “separation of concerns between content and presentation”.
articles.author: Geoff Graham

articles.title: ▶  Hello My Name Is St�phanie: A Talk On "Special Characters", Inclusive Design and User Experience
articles.description: A roughly 15-minute talk on how encoding and database decisions about formats and characters can result in an awful user experience.
articles.author: Stéphanie Walter

articles.title: The Hidden Cost of AI Coding
articles.description: No matter if you’re all in on vibe coding, or find AI development somewhat questionable, this article ponders the joy of the craft. Some food for thought.
articles.author: Matheus Lima

articles.title: Blob Shape with Hover Effect
articles.description: It’s Chrome only for now, but Temani quickly shares a technique for creating CSS-only single-liner blob shapes using clip-path and shape(). There’s a generator too if you want to make your own.
articles.author: Temani Afif

articles.title: Responsive Typographic Scale Generator
articles.description: Output is in standard CSS or Sass/SCSS and you can use rem or px for the font sizes using the clamp() function.
articles.author: DK Web Solutions

articles.title: CSS Editor: An Interactive Visual CSS Editor
articles.description: Nothing extraordinary here, but a nice way to use sliders and dropdowns to edit CSS on an element live on the page, with both 2D and 3D properties available.
articles.author: Ciprian Popescu

articles.title: Storybook 9 Beta
articles.description: The popular UI 'workshop' hits v9, with this beta that introduces visual testing, accessibility testing out of the box, component test widgets and more.
articles.author: Michael Shilman

articles.title: Annotation Mono: A Lovingly Crafted Handwriting-Style Monospace Font
articles.description: A legible, variable handwritten typeface inspired by monospace bitmap fonts — could be useful not just in your IDE, but may also work in any informal context, such as labels, headings, etc. Repo here.
articles.author: Qwerasd

articles.title: track-list: A Web Component to Enhance a List of Audio Tracks with Playlist Controls
articles.description: A neat idea to encapsulate a common UI pattern where a single track plays followed by each subsequent track in the list, with play/pause and next/previous functionality. Some demos here.
articles.author: Miriam Suzanne

articles.title: Design Tokens Name Generator
articles.description: For when you need to get started quickly, this tool will create ready-made naming conventions for your design system.
articles.author: Romina Kavcic

articles.title: Draggable Carousel: A Draggable Carousel/Slider Component for React, Vue, or Vanila JS
articles.description: Try it out on this Stackblitz demo.
articles.author: David Bismut
